title: dryopteris filix-mas subsp. caucasica
intro: Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. caucasica, also known as the Caucasian lady fern, is a subspecies of the common male fern (Dryopteris filix-mas) native to the Caucasus region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Dryopteris filix-mas subsp. caucasica include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
